text: The church was chosen by God as His mission agent in the dispensation of grace that He might manifest in this world the love of God to all people in a cross-cultural mission program. The missio ecclesiae in cross-cultural missions is thus reduced to evangelism in line with the text of Matthew 28:19-20, which is widely held by Evangelicals and Pentecostals. But when looking into other scripture passages, one will discover that there are many other areas where the local church is also involved in cross-cultural missions. Using thematic analysis to Romans 15:22-33 through a missionary perspective, the church can participate in cross-cultural missions by sharing power as missionaries that accompany evangelists on the mission field, as those who donate money in order to provide physical humanitarian assistance to the mission in which they pray for the evangelistic task. Therefore, the involvement of the local church is holistic — it is involved in both spiritual and physical aspects of mission.
explanation: The text is cohesive and uses formal academic phrasing typical of theological writing; while there are no clear errors, the measured, generalized analysis could be produced by AI as well as by a human author.
